title: ActiveCampaign partner/reseller here-- abandoned after 5 years of being a partner.
text:
ActiveCampaign partner/reseller here (5+ years). On Feb 21, 2026, my Partner Portal login stopped working after what appears to be a login-flow change. I followed all standard reset/recovery steps and the partner login paths support provided—but I’m stuck because I don’t know which username/email is tied to my Partner Portal identity, and recovery loops without resolving it. Support sent a list of my activehosted accounts, but that doesn’t identify the partner portal credential record. Work is now blocked and tasks are piling up.
If anyone at ActiveCampaign can help, I need: (1) confirmation of the partner portal email/username on file, and (2) a true partner credential reset path. Ticket: #11067128.

title: Stay Away!
text: No support. No response to emails.  This is just terrible. This seems like a cash grab where they overpromise and just sell.  Stay away. We are stuck, you don't have to be. No cross object reporting, no duplicate emails on different accounts, no duplicate account names. All promised before we came aboard.  Terrible company and terrible customer service. I mean ZERO responses to emails.

title: Really (really) poor customer service
text: Really (really) poor customer service. I was charged multiple times, and support was unprofessional.

title: Wanted to leave a review as I thought…
text: Wanted to leave a review as I thought this was top-notch customer service.  Our firm had changed its text act and we were no longer using active campaign. Our subscription was still on and their customer service handled it with care and resolved everything.  Andrea A. Our Rep walked us through everything connected us with management and resolved this in less than a day. We are super grateful and just happy to work with them and will recommend them for anyone going forward.

title: Horrible
text: Horrible. Do not use this service. They will not cancel your account and keep charging you. Find better alternatives. You can see the reviews on Trust Pilot for a reason. Run don’t walk. Look up my name and the 100 emails sent back and forth. You’ll find me.

title: I’ve been using ActiveCampaign for…
text:
I’ve been using ActiveCampaign for years, but the recent experience has been extremely frustrating.

Since early November, a critical bug has been blocking one of our core automations. I opened a ticket immediately, provided all the details, logs, and examples requested and after that? Weeks of silence, generic replies, and zero progress.

What makes it worse is the lack of accountability: every follow-up is met with “we’re looking into it,” but no ETA, no workaround, no ownership. For a platform positioned as “pro-level” and used for business-critical flows, this level of support is simply unacceptable.

At this point, the issue has been open for months with no fix. It’s creating operational delays and forcing us to look at alternative tools.

I really hope this review pushes the team to take support and reliability more seriously. A platform this costly shouldn’t break basic features and definitely shouldn’t leave customers hanging.

title: The best for automated email campaigns
text: ActiveCampaign has a steep learning curve, and I was a little initmidated. But I found all the training videos and support really helpful. I was able to become effective with the platform pretty quickly. I think the capabilities of the platform are awesome once you put in the time to learn the system. It's worth the time and the price to develop effective automated email campaigns, and AC is the best for automated email campaigns.

title: I really enjoy working with Active…
text:
I really enjoy working with Active Campaign. It is very user friendly, really intuitive. Plus when I haven´t been able to find a feature that I´m looking for, the customer service is very helpful. 
The multiple features allow me to develop highly attractive emails that still keep a great deal of professionalism.
I love all that I can have access to all the data and stats that help me making better decisions each time, as they reveal the bahaviour of the potential clients.

title: I signed up for AC and had to wait a…
text: I signed up for AC and had to wait a week before I could get an integration/onboarding call. I emailed the salesperson who signed me up twice and then left him a voicemail the next day to see if he could help me get a few questions answered so I wouldn't have to wait a week. Guy ghosted me, did not even acknowledge my call or emails. Even if he lacked the technical expertise to assist me, he should at least have gotten back to me. Unimpressed with the company so far. It is on a month-to-month contract. I will probably look for another platform with better customer service. That is not just poor customer service, it is downright poor manners.

title: ActiveCampaign has a lots of functions, sometimes performance issues
text:
ActiveCampaign is very mighty, has a lot of potential that I am actually using - not everything though because it is a bit tricky to stay on track with all the news and updates.

However, AC has some big disadvantages, such as:
- sometimes poor perfomance. Writing a mail and suddenly all is gone - very frustrating. 
- Had to pay an email expert because my mails very often landed in the spam (not caused by my mails but by the technical dmarc and some things that are related to AC, e.g. reputation of shared IPs). 
- Dashboard changes quite a lot times, it is very confusing to always adapt to the new overview.

Customer service though is great and very fast and very good and still, the different functions AC has are the biggest plus.

title: Good enough software for mailing lists
text: Good enough software for mailing lists. They've recently 'implemented AI' which means they've replaced all of the homepages with a text box. Their AI chatbot can direct me how to find things that were hidden, at least.

title: We're a client paying over 6000 euros…
text:
We're a client paying over 6000 euros per year, got an email to talk about renewal. So I answered to make one. Just 15 minutes before the meeting it got canceled without any communication around it. 
Emailing for 2 weeks already, got over 20 emails mostly written by AI agents. Sometimes a real person answers, saying things that doesn't make sense. Then, when I email back, I'll be put in the AI agent loop again. And... still no appointment.

title: ActiveCampaign - powerful and feature rich!
text:
I have been using ActiveCampaign for 7 years and over that time have saved hundreds of hours thanks to the powerful automations that can be built in ActiveCampaign. 

I continue to be impressed with ActiveCampaign's growth as a platform and the features that are regularly added tot he platform. 

Their support has been excellent, professional and timely.

Currently, the EDM functionality is what I use the least. It is excellent and powerful and the fact that our business systems are driven by the other features available is testimony to the power and functionality built into ActiveCampaign.

title: Very difficult to downgrade your account
text:
Nearly impossible to figure out how to downgrade your account. 

I go from talking to an AI agent to help articles that are no longer active. You have to jump on a call, apparently, to even be able to downgrade your account and haven't figured out how to do that. 

I just get very annoyed with services that intentionally build in sludge, and then, as a consumer, I need to spend a good amount of time being a detective to figure out how to do business with this service. It has me thinking about just canceling my account altogether. 

Overall, the service itself has been fine (that's the two-star rating), but I just find it very frustrating that you just can't get to a person and figure this out easily.

title: In general very time efficient and easy…
text: In general very time efficient and easy service to use with a great support from the team.

title: This is a great campaign site
text: This is a great campaign site. I hadn't heard of it before someone recommended this on LinkedIn. We are a mid-sized business, so it's great for our needs.

title: Reporting limitations and lack of customer support
text:
The platform is adequate for our basic day-to-day use and meets core functionality needs. However, the reporting capabilities are very limited and do not meet expectations.

We upgraded our plan specifically to gain access to more advanced reporting, but unfortunately, this did not result in any meaningful improvement or added functionality for our use case.

Customer support has been a significant issue. We have submitted numerous support tickets over an extended period of time and have never received follow-up, resolution, or a returned call. When calling the customer service number, all extensions lead to voicemail, and messages are not returned. There also does not appear to be an accessible or responsive account manager available to assist with ongoing issues.

Overall, the platform functions at a basic level, but reporting and customer support need substantial improvement.

title: You asked me to leave an honest review…
text:
You asked me to leave an honest review - I appreciate that and I hope that you use the reviews for good.  
I have been with AC for a good while now and do not use many of the features.  I have found it tricky to use and the help not very helpful.  It is pricey for what I need and the next step of number of contacts means that I will need to leave.  Sorry.  There is so much competition out there now - AC needs to improve.

title: Easy to to use and intuitive CRM tool…
text: Easy to to use and intuitive CRM tool for small and medium sized businesses.

title: I had a very negative experience with…
text:
I had a very negative experience with ActiveCampaign’s affiliate program. I was running traffic on a direct ActiveCampaign affiliate account for a long time. After the program was migrated to PartnerStack, my affiliate account was suddenly locked without clear prior notice, and outstanding commissions were not paid.

There was no transparent explanation, no proper transition support, and communication became extremely slow. As an affiliate, this caused direct financial loss and serious disruption to my business. I also felt ongoing pressure around payment issues, while my own unpaid commissions remained unresolved.

This experience shows a lack of accountability and poor handling of affiliate partners. Until ActiveCampaign addresses this issue and provides a clear resolution, I cannot recommend promoting their products or joining their affiliate program.

title: Still satisfied but pricey
text: We have been using Active Campaign for about 4 years now and like most of the features. Setting up and managing customers/contacts is easy as is newsletter creation...most of the time. It seems to be getting quite expensive even though we are not adding customers beyond our tier.

title: Good tech support
text: I always look for great tech support, which is missing from so many platforms these days. Active Campaign has been good in that respect. It's a program with many capabilities I'm not taking advantage of.

title: Shocking experience
text:
Shocking experience. buggy af. They gaslight you when you complain to customer service about why a pre scheduled  email never sent or why an email you tested 3 times (and had perfect fonts) sent with whack fonts and invisible text 
Absolute disaster
We have literally just moved platforms this month after years of complaining and no relief or u detaining
It’s 2025 and their ux feels like 1980s
Trust me you can do better 🙌🏻

title: STAY AWAY before being locked-in
text:
As you can see from many other answers, people who've recently joined and indeed in the beginning you get lot's of support till you are locked-in. Once in they charge outrages prices for the amount of contacts regardless if you are actively mailing those contacts or not. 

Also the platform feels outdated, and full of bugs, not at all in relation to what you pay / month. 
We're currently paying 500 euro/month for 15K contacts and really aim to keep our list as clean as possible, in order not to have those increased charges. 

Thanks to these reviews, we get contacted by alternative services.

title: Very difficult to downgrade your account
text:
Nearly impossible to figure out how to downgrade your account. 

I go from talking to an AI agent to help articles that are no longer active. You have to jump on a call, apparently, to even be able to downgrade your account and haven't figured out how to do that. 

I just get very annoyed with services that intentionally build in sludge, and then, as a consumer, I need to spend a good amount of time being a detective to figure out how to do business with this service. It has me thinking about just canceling my account altogether. 

Overall, the service itself has been fine (that's the two-star rating), but I just find it very frustrating that you just can't get to a person and figure this out easily.

title: I am a small business owner and I have…
text:
I am a small business owner and I have been an AC for over 5 years now. What I like is the simplicity, the price point ($250/mth vs. a HubSpot $2500), the connection with Zapier for automations and data sharing with other subscriptions I have (e.g. website & Calendly) and the Email marketing feature, especially the design. 

However, I am often annoyed by its clunkiness; sometimes it doesn't safe, an error in the system that my team has flagged with AC years ago is still not fixed, the look and feel is like the 80s, the performance (speed) needs improvement, and the yearly price jumps are super annoying. 

But at the end of the day, one needs to decide price vs functionality & user experience - it still does a good job.

title: We just moved to AC
text: We just moved to AC, so we will see how it works out. So far, fairly good in terms of the platform not being buggy, the editor working pretty well, and the folks that work there have been nice. Only thing is after moving our sender reputation immediately dropped and our emails stopped getting as many opens. . . so not entirely sure what to do there. Tried to talk to support about it but it seems they think it's outside of their control? We moved from Mailchimp where we could never get in touch with support whatsoever so this is an upgrade.

title: Knowledgeable and helpful staff
text: Had an excellent experience reviewing our current plan with ActiveCampaign (Thomas B).  He took the time to understand how we are using the platform and suggested a modified plan to align with our size and marketing goals. He thoroughly answered questions on improving data analyses, automations, and other AI questions. Although it sometimes feels like it's hard to get through to a live person for help,  when we can set something up, we have always been impressed with the outcome and quality of their staff.

title: ActiveCampaign has excellent customer…
text: ActiveCampaign has excellent customer service. Their team is responsive, knowledgeable, and always willing to go the extra mile to resolve issues quickly. The support feels personal, and they make sure you walk away with clear answers and solutions. It really makes using the platform smooth and stress-free.

title: The platform is good and functionality…
text: The platform is good and functionality served me for more trhan 10 years. I has now become too expensive for my family based business, they suddenly doubled my monthly premium in 2023 just for adding a feature I am not using and is unable to offer a smaller package, although they say the price offered is at 68% discount.  Ridiculous! Im not stupid.  I'm moving away as soon as this term is over to Brevo at a fraction of the cost for the same automation functions suitable for me.

title: Stay away if you can
text:
We've been using ActiveCampaign for several years and are getting a lot of frustration from their services. They lock you in and then continuously charge you more and more. Recently they have charged us for a feature we specifically said we didn't need, and now they don't even bother issuing a full refund for that.

From a feature point of view, the platform is very slow. Also, over the years there were many instances of contacts being stuck forever at wait steps in automations. This was a waste of ad spend and also created lots of operational issues.

We are seriously looking at alternatives.

title: Intuitive, excellent customer support, affordable
text: The price is very good. The customer service, especially for what we pay, is generous. The platform is easy to use. I used it first many years ago and chose it for a new project because it was so easy to learn and intuitive. Its easy to integrate with Wordpress and other platforms, too (though it does take some know-how).

title: Active Campaign is consistently at the…
text: Active Campaign is consistently at the top of the list for email automation and deliverability. It has a no-frills CRM to keep account notes as well. They have a new text message marketing system as well. Great bang for the buck.

title: They gave me great support
text: They gave me great support. I actually don't use them anymore because I don't need a software that robust right now, but when I was with them, they gave me excellent customer support. I would definitely work with them again.

title: Super Opaque Pricing
text:
Trying to make pricing as opaque as possible. They tried to push through a huge price increase at renewal (+35%) but act like they're doing you a favour because actually "you're getting a 35% discount on list price". 

I decided to downgrade my plan to a level that costs $2,400 on their website pricing page but the account rep tried to sell me the same plan with a "custom offer" for $5,000

Unless its cancelling or downgrading, they won't respond to anything you contact them with.

title: Be careful depending your business in active campaign
text:
Used to be great now every single additional added function or every new contact will add hundreds to your costs.

This is not how you increase revenue. They could have introduced new features and then upsell those but they keep taking away functions from their key services.

I honestly regret having my clients on active campaign and I wish I went with an automation platform that doesn’t keep squeezing you for money for every single new function.

title: Great marketing and CRM tool
text:
Great marketing and CRM tool! 

We are working with Active Campaign for quite some years now. It's very user friendly and it has lots of possibilities. We are mainly using it for CRM and for mailings.

title: Scammers
text:
Scammers. They deleted - without notice and without any explanation - my affiliate account with over 3000$ dollars earned. My business is in the supplement and pet space so 100% compliant. Plus they overcharged me in another account and refuse to give me money back. I've been using Activecampaign for over 10 years, this company is not what it used to be anymore.

EDIT: they just replied saying they "have reached out", but obviously they did not.

title: Highly recommend Active Campaign for your Business!
text: I have been with Active Campaign for quite a while now (years) and they are very helpful and a pleasure to deal with. At one time I had to set up a huge campaign and I really wasn't sure about certain aspects and they guided with all the steps that I needed, it was such a relief knowing that I had someone on the other end of the computer to help me. I would highly recommend Active Campaign for your business.

title: Very disappointing experience
text:
My team has been using ActiveCampaign for about six months now, and I must say it has been extremely disappointing.
There are so many missing features and broken elements in the CRM that I don’t even know where to start.
If you need support, you will be met with the most basic, AI-generated questions I have seen so far, and only if you insist on speaking to a real human can you do so.
It feels like someone had an idea like: “Hey guys, how about we make a complete CRM system online?” — then they built the shell of a CRM… and stopped.
From my experience with all the other competitors in the market, I would strongly advise you to look elsewhere.
